notes: got the compaction test results back from the geotech. first layer at 300 mil depth came in at 97 percent MDD and the second layer at 150 came in at 101 percent. both pass the minimum 95 so were good
concrete spec for tomorrows pour is 40 MPA at 28 days, 120 slump, 20 mil aggregate, S40 exposure class. batch plant confirmed the mix design so we're all set there
checked the steel delivery against the schedule. 16 N16 bars at 6 metres, 24 N12 bars also at 6 metres, and 45 N12 ligatures at 300 centres. all matches up, mill certs are in the folder

went through the window schedule for level 1. windows 1 through 8 are all 2100 by 1200 awning type, and windows 9 and 10 are the smaller highlight ones 1800 by 600. all aluminium powdercoat in monument colour

the stormwater pipe is 150 mil PVC SN8 class, laid at 1 in 80 fall running towards the junction pit on the north east corner. pipe covers sitting at 450 mil which is above the 300 minimum so thats fine
slab is 200 mil thick with N12 reo at 200 centres each way top and bottom. engineers checked the reo yesterday and tagged it off. mesh chairs at 1200 centres holding up the top mat

checked the structural steel connection on column B3 to beam BM-04. six M20 bolts 8.8 grade per connection, all torqued to 340 newton metres as per the engineers spec. torque marks are all done and painted
